perturbation.original_text: 1. Subject to the limitations set forth herein, “ Confidential Information ” means any and all confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of Disclo sing Party (whether oral, written, electronic or otherwise), including but not limited to marketing and sales data, customer lists, customer requirements, price lists, produc t and service lists, market st udies, financial statements, business plans, the names and backgrounds of key pe rsonnel, research and developmen t, service methods, processes, improvements, devices, know-how, inventions, discoveries, conce pts, ideas, designs, methods and information, and all related notes, analyses, compilations, stu dies, specifications, designs, plans, enhancements, data of Disclosing Party and any notes or other m aterials prepared by Receiving Party or any of its partners, directors, officers, employees, agents, co ntractors, affiliates, attorneys and other advisors or representatives (collectively, “ Representatives ”) that contain or are generated from such information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Subject to the limitations set forth herein, “ Confidential Information ” means only that information explicitly labeled as such in writing by the Disclosing Party. This definition excludes, but is not limited to, marketing and sales data, customer lists, customer requirements, price lists, product and service lists, market studies, financial statements, business plans, the names and backgrounds of key personnel, research and development, service methods, processes, improvements, devices, know-how, inventions, discoveries, concepts, ideas, designs, methods and information, and all related notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans, enhancements, data of Disclosing Party and any notes or other materials prepared by Receiving Party or any of its partners, directors, officers, employees, agents, contractors, affiliates, attorneys and other advisors or representatives (collectively, “ Representatives ”) that contain or are generated from such information.
perturbation.explanation: By narrowly defining 'Confidential Information' to include only explicitly labeled information and excluding a broad range of data typically considered confidential, the contract creates a loophole that could allow for the misuse of sensitive data. This goes against common trade secret and confidentiality practices.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified definition contradicts the UTSA's definition of 'trade secret', which includes a broad range of information that derives independent economic value from not being generally known or readily ascertainable. The modified text limits it to ONLY 'explicitly labeled' data, and explicitly excludes other types of data that is traditionally held as trade secrets.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 404 | Berkman Klein Center Skip to the main content Page Cannot Be Found Brave traveler, your journey on our site has brought you to an abrupt dead end. Perhaps you wish to explore one of these other options? About Us Get Involved Events Community Publications Projects & Tools Last updated Mar 27, 2024
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information', requiring it to be explicitly labeled as such in writing, while also excluding data typically considered confidential. The Law Explanation states that this contradicts the UTSA's broad definition of 'trade secret', as it limits confidential information to 'explicitly labeled' data and excludes other types of data traditionally considered trade secrets. Although there are no valid law snippets, the Law Explanation strongly suggests that the modified definition undermines the protection of trade secrets as understood by the UTSA.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Upon receipt of any Confidential Information, Receiving Part y shall: (a) only disclose such Confidential Information to those of its Representatives with a need to have access to and knowledge of the Confidential Inform ation for the limited purpose expressly stated herein and not disclose to any other person ex cept with the prior written approval of Disclosing Party; (b) advise any of its Representatives who receive the Confident ial Information of the existence and terms of this Agreement and of the obligations of confidentiality and non- disclosure herein; cause its Representatives to comply with thi s Agreement as if they were parties hereto; and be responsible for a ny breach of this Agreement by its Representatives; (c) use and require its Representatives to use at least the sam e degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as they use to protect the ir own proprietary information, which degree of care shall not be less than reasonable care; an d
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Upon receipt of any Confidential Information, Receiving Part y shall: (a) only disclose such Confidential Information to those of its Representatives with a need to have access to and knowledge of the Confidential Inform ation for any purpose they deem appropriate and not disclose to any other person ex cept with the prior written approval of Disclosing Party; (b) advise any of its Representatives who receive the Confident ial Information of the existence and terms of this Agreement, but representatives are not bound by the agreement; cause its Representatives to comply with thi s Agreement as if they were parties hereto; and be responsible for a ny breach of this Agreement by its Representatives; (c) use and require its Representatives to use at least the sam e degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as they use to protect the ir own proprietary information, which degree of care shall not be less than reasonable care; an d
perturbation.explanation: By allowing Representatives to use confidential information for "any purpose they deem appropriate," the contract negates the purpose of the NDA. And saying representatives are not bound by the agreement creates a contradiction because section 2b requires the representatives to comply with the agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification could potentially violate the Economic Espionage Act, which prohibits the unauthorized disclosure of trade secrets with the intent to convert them to the economic benefit of anyone other than the owner. The new language allows 'Representatives' to use the data for any purpose and Representatives are not bound by the agreement, directly contradicting the Act's intent.

Theft of trade secrets (a) Whoever, with intent to convert a trade secret, that is related to a product or service used in or intended for use in interstate or foreign commerce, to the economic benefit of anyone other than the owner thereof, and intending or knowing that the offense will, injure any owner of that trade secret, knowingly- (1) steals, or without authorization appropriates, takes, carries away, or conceals, or by fraud, artifice, or decep
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a legal contradiction. The original agreement ensures representatives comply with confidentiality, while the changed text states representatives are "not bound by the agreement" but are still expected to comply, introducing conflicting obligations. The modification permitting representatives to use confidential information for "any purpose they deem appropriate" directly opposes the intent of the Economic Espionage Act, which aims to prevent unauthorized use of trade secrets for economic benefit, as described in the Law Explanation. Snippet 2, referencing 18 USC 1832, "Theft of trade secrets," emphasizes the illegality of converting trade secrets for the benefit of someone other than the owner, which is precisely what the modified contract allows representatives to do. This deviation from legally recognized confidentiality makes the contract unenforceable and thus a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 8. At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receivi ng Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, immedi ately return (or, at the op tion of Disclosing Party, destroy) all tangible or electronic Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving P arty or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductio ns of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans , enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Part y further agrees to promptly provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or co ntrol of any Confidential Information. Provided, however, that Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information, subject to the restrictions on use and disclosure contained in this Agreement, in accordance
perturbation.changed_text: 8. At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receivi ng Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, immedi ately return (or, at the op tion of Disclosing Party, destroy) all tangible or electronic Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving P arty or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductio ns of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans , enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Part y further agrees to promptly provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or co ntrol of any Confidential Information. Provided, however, that Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information, without restriction to use and disclosure contained in this Agreement, in accordance
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'subject to the restrictions on use and disclosure' to 'without restriction to use and disclosure' it goes against the spirit of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law - Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: This contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing present in all contracts. By allowing the Receiving Party to retain copies of Confidential Information without any restrictions, the modification effectively nullifies the confidentiality obligations agreed upon earlier in the contract, undermining the Disclosing Party's reasonable expectations and the contract's purpose.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: good faith | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you good faith Good faith is a broad term that’s used to encompass honest dealing. Depending on the exact setting, good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties , observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent . A fiduciary relationship creates a duty of good faith between the agent and the principal . The breach of this duty of good faith can lead to liability . Failure to act in good faith is known as bad faith and is generally considered to be a level of culpability greater than negligence . [Last reviewed in January of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE business law contracts wex definitions business sectors legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modification, by removing restrictions on the use and disclosure of confidential information, contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, which requires 'faithful performance of duties' and 'observance of fair dealing standards,' as stated in Scraped Law Snippet 1. Removing restrictions allows the Receiving Party to potentially exploit confidential information, undermining the Disclosing Party's reasonable expectations and negating the contract's purpose of maintaining confidentiality. The changed text essentially nullifies a key obligation, resulting in a direct conflict with the principle of acting in good faith.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Receiving Party acknowledges that any disclosure or misappr opriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cau se Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, a grees that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief in addition to other remedies and relief t hat would be available at law or in equity without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting a bo nd. In the event of any action for breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party or its Representatives, th e prevailing party shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses incurred in such action.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Receiving Party acknowledges that any disclosure or misappr opriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cau se Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, a grees that Disclosing Party shall not be entitled to injunctive relief but is limited to monetary damages, and only if actual damages are proven in court. In the event of any action for breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party or its Representatives, th e prevailing party shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses incurred in such action.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the right to injunctive relief significantly weakens the Disclosing Party's ability to protect their confidential information quickly and effectively. It forces them to rely solely on proving monetary damages, which can be challenging and time-consuming, potentially allowing further harm to occur in the meantime.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification removes the availability of injunctive relief, directly contradicting the established precedent for using injunctive relief in trade secret disputes, particularly as outlined in the Uniform Trade Secrets Act (UTSA). The UTSA allows for injunctive relief to prevent further misappropriation of trade secrets, which this modification now prohibits.
perturbation.justification: The modification removes the availability of injunctive relief, which weakens the Disclosing Party's ability to protect their confidential information. The Law Explanation states that this contradicts the established precedent for using injunctive relief in trade secret disputes, particularly as outlined in the Uniform Trade Secrets Act (UTSA), since UTSA allows for injunctive relief to prevent further misappropriation of trade secrets, which this modification now prohibits. While no law snippets were provided, the Perturbation Explanation states it forces them to rely solely on proving monetary damages, which can be challenging and time-consuming, potentially allowing further harm to occur in the meantime. Therefore, the removal of injunctive relief represents a significant departure from standard legal remedies in trade secret cases and contradicts the intent to prevent misappropriation.

perturbation.original_text: 15. This Agreement shall be gove rned by and construed in accord ance with the domestic, substantive laws of the State of New York, without regard to co nflicts of laws principles. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to its subject matter. This Agreement may be waived, amended or modified only by an instrum ent in writing signed by the party against which such waiver, amendment or modification is sought to be enforced. The term “person” as used in this Agreement shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any individual, group or entity. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereo f preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereun der. This Agreement is not assignable and is binding on any successors of Receiving Party. If any provis ion of this Agreement is for any reason adjudged to be void, invalid or unenforceable, then the remaind er of this Agreement shall continue and remain in full force and effect. This Agreement may be execute d in counterpart by facsimile, PDF or electronic signature, which shall be deemed to be an original f or evidentiary purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. This Agreement shall be gove rned by and construed in accord ance with the laws of Lichtenstein, without regard to co nflicts of laws principles. This Agreement constitutes only a partial agreement of the parties with respect to its subject matter. This Agreement may be waived orally, and can be amended via email. The term “person” as used in this Agreement shall ONLY include humans. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereo f preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereun der. This Agreement is freely assignable and is not binding on any successors of Receiving Party. If any provis ion of this Agreement is for any reason adjudged to be void, invalid or unenforceable, then the remaind er of this Agreement shall continue and remain in full force and effect. This Agreement may be execute d in counterpart by facsimile, PDF or electronic signature, which shall be deemed to be an original f or evidentiary purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This section makes multiple changes. 1. It changes the governing law to Lichtenstein, where NY law was previously specified. 2. Saying that the agreement is only partial is a contradiction. 3. It says the agreement may be waived orally. 4. By stating that the term 'person' shall ONLY include humans goes against the legal precedent that a person can be a business or other legal entity.
perturbation.law_explanation: The definition of 'person' as only including humans directly contradicts the established legal definition, which includes corporations, companies, associations, firms, partnerships, societies, and joint stock companies, as well as individuals. By limiting the definition, the contract could be interpreted to exclude liability for breaches committed by non-human entities.

Words denoting number, gender, and so forth In determining the meaning of any Act of Congress, unless the context indicates otherwise- words importing the singular include and apply to several persons, parties, or things; words importing the plural include the singular; words importing the masculine gender include the feminine as well; words used in the present tense include the future as well as the present; the words "insane" and "insane person
perturbation.justification: The modification to the contract text, specifically limiting the definition of 'person' to ONLY include humans, contradicts established legal definitions. The Law Explanation indicates this conflicts with standard legal interpretations, which include corporations, companies, associations, firms, partnerships, societies, and joint stock companies. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 1 USC 1, states that 'words importing the singular include and apply to several persons, parties, or things,' suggesting a broader interpretation of 'person' than solely humans. This significantly weakens the contract's enforceability against non-human entities, creating a direct legal contradiction.
